possible_species.name: Epipremnum aureum (Golden, Marble Queen, N'Joy, Manjula, Neon, Pearls & Jade, Cebu Blue)
Scindapsus pictus (Silver Splash, Exotica, Jade Satin)

disambiguation_records.disambiguation_question: Just to make sure I pull the right info — is your plant solid green or neon yellow, classically marbled white, or does it have more of a silvery/metallic sheen to the leaves?

visual_cues.Epipremnum aureum: heart-shaped leaves, matte finish, variegation in white, yellow, or lime green
visual_cues.Scindapsus pictus: slightly thicker leaves, distinctly silver or metallic splotching, velvety texture
visual_cues.Scindapsus treubii: narrow elongated leaves, very dark green to near-black, minimal pattern

disambiguation_records.care_divergence_notes: Epipremnum is more forgiving with water and light; Scindapsus prefers to dry out more and hates overwatering more aggressively. Not night-and-day but meaningful for fertilizer cadence.
disambiguation_records.reclassification_note: True botanical 'Pothos' is a separate genus entirely — rarely sold as houseplants. Everything the hobby calls 'pothos' is Epipremnum or Scindapsus.

disambiguation_records.disambiguation_question: Prayer plants are a whole family — can you describe the leaf pattern? Red or pink veins point one direction, bold pinstripes another, and rattlesnake-style spots are something else entirely.

visual_cues.Maranta leuconeura kerchoveana: pale green with dark brown blotches along midrib — 'rabbit tracks'
visual_cues.Maranta leuconeura erythroneura: deep green with vivid red veins, herringbone pattern
visual_cues.Goeppertia ornata: dark green with thin pink/white pinstripes radiating from midrib
visual_cues.Goeppertia lancifolia: long narrow leaves, dark oval spots on light green — rattlesnake
visual_cues.Goeppertia roseopicta: broad oval, bold concentric ring pattern, often with pink blush
visual_cues.Stromanthe sanguinea: lance-shaped, cream and green variegation on top, magenta underside

disambiguation_records.care_divergence_notes: True Maranta is slightly more forgiving than Goeppertia. Stromanthe is the diva of the group. All share humidity preference and dislike of fluoride/chlorine in tap water.
disambiguation_records.reclassification_note: Most plants sold as 'Calathea' are now correctly classified as Goeppertia following a 2012 reclassification. Nursery tags still widely use Calathea. Maranta is a distinct genus in the same family (Marantaceae) — not a Calathea at all.
disambiguation_records.source_reference: Marantaceae identification print — The Smart Local

possible_species.name: Ficus elastica (Rubber Plant — Burgundy, Ruby, Tineke)

disambiguation_records.disambiguation_question: Ficus is a big genus with pretty different care needs depending on which one you have. Does it have huge violin-shaped leaves, wide waxy burgundy/green leaves, small glossy leaves on drooping branches, or something else?

visual_cues.Ficus lyrata: very large, violin/fiddle-shaped leaves, wavy edges, prominent veining
visual_cues.Ficus elastica: large oval leaves, thick and waxy, often deep burgundy or variegated cream
visual_cues.Ficus benjamina: small ovate leaves, arching/weeping branch structure, prolific leaf drop when stressed
visual_cues.Ficus triangularis: small triangular to heart-shaped leaves, compact growth
visual_cues.Ficus pumila: tiny rounded leaves on vining/creeping stems, completely different habit — groundcover or wall creeper, not a specimen plant

disambiguation_records.care_divergence_notes: Lyrata needs bright indirect light and hates being moved. Benjamina drops leaves at the slightest draft or relocation. Elastica is the most forgiving of the group. Pumila is a completely different use case.
